Blockpack Docs GitHub →
BLOCKPACK
Overview
Home File Format
Query Paths
Filter Query Intrinsic Fast-Path Trace by ID Metrics Query
Write Path
Write Pipeline
Indexes
KLL Sketches Count-Min Sketch TopK HyperLogLog Bloom Filters Intrinsic Columns
Blocks
Block Scoring Block Format
Performance
Benchmarks Production Results

File Format

Blockpack is a columnar binary format for OpenTelemetry trace and log data. A single .blockpack file is self-contained: it holds block payloads, all index structures, and metadata needed to answer any query without an external catalog. All multi-byte integers are little-endian unless noted otherwise. All offsets are absolute byte positions within the file.

Overview

Read-from-Footer Model

A reader cannot locate any data without first reading the footer at the known position file_size - 22 (or file_size - 34 for footer v4). The footer gives the header_offset; the header gives metadata_offset and metadata_len; the metadata section contains the Block Index which gives each block's byte offset and length. There is no seekable structure at byte 0.

This model also allows a streaming writer: blocks are written forward as they are finalized, indexes are appended afterward, and no backward seeks are ever needed.

Endianness and Encoding

  • All integers: little-endian
  • Timestamps: Unix nanoseconds as uint64
  • Trace IDs and span IDs: raw 16-byte and 8-byte big-endian binary (as stored in OTLP proto)
  • Floating point: IEEE 754, stored as uint64 bits little-endian
  • Block payloads: compressed with Zstd at write time
  • Metadata section (v12+): snappy-compressed on disk; decompress before parsing

Signal Types

Signalsignal_type byteNotes
Trace spans0x01Default; file header v10/v11/v12/v13
Log records0x02Requires file header version 12+

Version History

VersionChange
v10Baseline format: block header, column encoding, metadata, compact trace index
v11Added kind byte to Block Index Entry (after length, before span_count)
v12Snappy-compressed metadata section; extended file header with signal_type byte at offset 21 (22-byte header)
v13Block Index Entry omits min_trace_id[16] + max_trace_id[16] (saves 32 bytes/block); footer V4 adds intrinsic index offset/len
Footer v322-byte footer: version[2] + header_offset[8] + compact_offset[8] + compact_len[4]; used by v10–v12 files
Footer v434-byte footer: v3 fields + intrinsic_index_offset[8] + intrinsic_index_len[4]; used by v13+ files
Block v12Block content format version 12: column metadata entries omit the 16-byte stats_offset/stats_len stub (saves 16 bytes per column per block); written by v13+ file writers

Complete File Layout

The following diagram shows the complete on-disk byte order for a v11/v12 file with all sections present.

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                       BLOCKPACK FILE FORMAT  (v11-v12 / footer v3)                  ║
╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  byte 0                                                                              ║
║  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║  │ BLOCK 0                                                                        │ ║
║  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │ ║
║  │  │ BLOCK HEADER  (24 bytes)                                                  │  │ ║
║  │  │  magic[4]=0xC011FEA1 · version[1] · reserved[3]                          │  │ ║
║  │  │  span_count[4] · col_count[4] · reserved2[8]                             │  │ ║
║  │  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤  │ ║
║  │  │ COLUMN METADATA  (col_count × entry)                                      │  │ ║
║  │  │  name_len[2] · name · type[1]                                             │  │ ║
║  │  │  data_offset[8] · data_len[8] · reserved[16] (stats_offset/len, always 0)│  │ ║
║  │  │  ↑ repeated col_count times, sorted by column name                        │  │ ║
║  │  │  (block v12: reserved[16] omitted — see §Block Payloads)                  │  │ ║
║  │  ├──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤  │ ║
║  │  │ COLUMN DATA  (one encoded blob per column)                                │  │ ║
║  │  │  enc_version[1]=2 · encoding_kind[1]                                      │  │ ║
║  │  │                                                                            │  │ ║
║  │  │  kind 1/2   Dictionary    index_width[1] · dict_zstd[4+N]                │  │ ║
║  │  │             row_count[4] · presence_rle[4+N] · indexes[N×width]          │  │ ║
║  │  │  kind 3/4   InlineBytes   row_count[4] · presence_rle[4+N]               │  │ ║
║  │  │             [present_count[4]] · per value: len[4] · bytes               │  │ ║
║  │  │  kind 5     DeltaUint64   span_count[4] · presence_rle[4+N]              │  │ ║
║  │  │             base[8] · width[1] · offsets_zstd[4+N]                       │  │ ║
║  │  │  kind 6/7   RLEIndexes    index_width[1] · dict_zstd[4+N]                │  │ ║
║  │  │             row_count[4] · presence_rle[4+N]                             │  │ ║
║  │  │             index_count[4] · rle_len[4] · rle_data[N]                    │  │ ║
║  │  │  kind 8/9   XORBytes      span_count[4] · presence_rle[4+N]              │  │ ║
║  │  │             xor_data_zstd[4+N]                                            │  │ ║
║  │  │  kind 10/11 PrefixBytes   span_count[4] · presence_rle[4+N]              │  │ ║
║  │  │             prefix_dict_zstd[4+N] · suffix_data_zstd[4+N]               │  │ ║
║  │  │  kind 12/13 DeltaDict     index_width[1] · dict_zstd[4+N]                │  │ ║
║  │  │             row_count[4] · presence_rle[4+N]                             │  │ ║
║  │  │             delta_indexes_zstd[4+N]                                       │  │ ║
║  │  │                                                                            │  │ ║
║  │  │  presence_rle = rle_len[4] · rle_data  (bitset: bit i set = row i present)│  │ ║
║  │  │  odd kinds = dense · even kinds = sparse (only present rows stored)        │  │ ║
║  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │ ║
║  ├─ BLOCK 1 (same structure) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤ ║
║  ├─ ...                                                                            │ ║
║  └─ BLOCK N-1 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ┘ ║
║                                                                                      ║
║  header_offset  ◄── stored in footer                                                ║
║  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║  │ FILE HEADER  (21 bytes v10/v11, 22 bytes v12+)                                 │ ║
║  │  magic[4]=0xC011FEA1 · version[1] · metadata_offset[8] · metadata_len[8]     │ ║
║  │  [v12+: signal_type[1] at offset 21]                                           │ ║
║  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ║
║                                                                                      ║
║  metadata_offset  ◄── from file header                                              ║
║  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║  │ METADATA SECTION  (metadata_len bytes)                                         │ ║
║  │  (V12+: entire section is snappy-compressed; decompress before parsing)         │ ║
║  │                                                                                 │ ║
║  │  ── BLOCK INDEX ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────    │ ║
║  │  block_count[4]                                                                 │ ║
║  │  per block entry:                                                               │ ║
║  │    offset[8] · length[8] · kind[1]  ← kind byte added in v11                  │ ║
║  │    span_count[4] · min_start[8] · max_start[8]                                 │ ║
║  │    min_trace_id[16] · max_trace_id[16]  ← omitted in v13+                      │ ║
║  │                                                                                 │ ║
║  │  ── RANGE INDEX ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────    │ ║
║  │  range_count[4]                                                                 │ ║
║  │  per range column:                                                              │ ║
║  │    name_len[2] · name · type[1]                                                 │ ║
║  │    bucket_min[8] · bucket_max[8] · boundary_count[4]                           │ ║
║  │    boundaries[N×8] · typed_count[4] · typed_boundaries…                        │ ║
║  │    value_count[4]                                                               │ ║
║  │    per value: value_key (type-encoded) · block_id_count[4] · block_ids[N×4]    │ ║
║  │                                                                                 │ ║
║  │  ── COLUMN INDEX (stub, always empty) ────────────────────────────────────    │ ║
║  │  per block: col_count[4] = 0                                                    │ ║
║  │                                                                                 │ ║
║  │  ── TRACE BLOCK INDEX ────────────────────────────────────────────────────    │ ║
║  │  fmt_version[1]=0x02 · trace_count[4]                                           │ ║
║  │  per trace (sorted by trace_id):                                                │ ║
║  │    trace_id[16] · block_count[2] · block_id[2] × N                             │ ║
║  │  (v1 legacy: block_id[2] · span_count[2] · span_indices[N×2] — readable)       │ ║
║  │                                                                                 │ ║
║  │  ── TS INDEX ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────    │ ║
║  │  magic[4]=0xC011FEED · version[1]=1 · count[4]                                  │ ║
║  │  per block (sorted by min_ts ascending):                                        │ ║
║  │    min_ts[8] · max_ts[8] · block_idx[4]                        (20 bytes ea)   │ ║
║  │                                                                                 │ ║
║  │  ── SKETCH INDEX (column-major; absent if no blocks) ────────────────────    │ ║
║  │  magic[4]=0x534B5443 ("SKTC") · num_blocks[4] · num_columns[4]                 │ ║
║  │  per column (sorted by name):                                                   │ ║
║  │    col_name_len[2] · col_name[N]                                                │ ║
║  │    presence[⌈num_blocks/8⌉ bytes]  (bitset: 1=column present in block)          │ ║
║  │    distinct[num_blocks × 4]  (uint32 per block, 0 if absent — HLL cardinality)  │ ║
║  │    topk_k[1]=20                                                                 │ ║
║  │    per present block: entry_count[1] × { fp[8 LE] · count[2 LE] }              │ ║
║  │    cms_depth[1]=4 · cms_width[2 LE]=64                                          │ ║
║  │    per present block: cms_data[depth × width × 2]  (512 bytes)                  │ ║
║  │    per present block: fuse_len[4 LE] · fuse_data[fuse_len]                      │ ║
║  │                                                                                 │ ║
║  │  ── FILE BLOOM (FBLM; optional, after sketch index) ─────────────────────    │ ║
║  │  magic[4 LE]=0x46424C4D ("FBLM") · version[1]=0x01 · col_count[4]              │ ║
║  │  per column (sorted by name):                                                   │ ║
║  │    name_len[2] · name[name_len] · fuse_len[4] · fuse_data[fuse_len]            │ ║
║  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ║
║                                                                                      ║
║  compact_offset  ◄── from footer  (absent if compact_len = 0)                       ║
║  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║  │ COMPACT TRACE INDEX                                                             │ ║
║  │  magic[4]=0xC01DC1DE · version[1]=2 · block_count[4]                           │ ║
║  │  bloom_bytes[4] · bloom_data[bloom_bytes]                                       │ ║
║  │  block_table: block_count × { file_offset[8] · file_length[4] }  (12 bytes ea) │ ║
║  │  fmt_version[1]=0x02 · trace_count[4]                                           │ ║
║  │  per trace: trace_id[16] · block_count[2] · block_id[2] × N                   │ ║
║  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ║
║                                                                                      ║
║  intrinsic_index_offset  ◄── from footer v4 only                                    ║
║  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║  │ INTRINSIC INDEX  (v13+ / footer v4 only)                                        │ ║
║  │  snappy-compressed TOC blob followed by per-column data pages                   │ ║
║  │  TOC: version[1]=1 · col_count[4] · per column:                                 │ ║
║  │    name_len[2] · name · col_type[1] · format[1]                                 │ ║
║  │    offset[8] · length[4] · entry_count[4]                                       │ ║
║  │    min_len[2] · min_val · max_len[2] · max_val                                  │ ║
║  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ║
║                                                                                      ║
║  file_size - 22  (footer v3, 22 bytes)  OR  file_size - 34  (footer v4, 34 bytes)  ║
║  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ ║
║  │ FOOTER  (22 bytes v3 / 34 bytes v4)                                             │ ║
║  │  v3: version[2]=3 · header_offset[8] · compact_offset[8] · compact_len[4]     │ ║
║  │  v4: version[2]=4 · header_offset[8] · compact_offset[8] · compact_len[4]     │ ║
║  │       intrinsic_index_offset[8] · intrinsic_index_len[4]                        │ ║
║  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Constants and Magic Numbers

MagicNumber        = 0xC011FEA1  // 4-byte file and block magic
CompactIndexMagic  = 0xC01DC1DE  // Compact trace index section magic
TSIndexMagic       = 0xC011FEED  // TS index section magic
SketchIndexMagic   = 0x534B5443  // "SKTC" — sketch index section magic
FileBloomMagic     = 0x46424C4D  // "FBLM" — file-level bloom section magic

VersionV10  uint8  = 10          // Block encoding version (current minimum)
VersionV11  uint8  = 11          // Adds Kind byte to block index entry
VersionV12  uint8  = 12          // Snappy metadata + signal_type byte in header
VersionV13  uint8  = 13          // Block index omits MinTraceID/MaxTraceID; footer V4; block content V12

SignalTypeTrace uint8 = 0x01     // File header: OTEL trace spans
SignalTypeLog   uint8 = 0x02     // File header: OTEL log records (requires v12+)

FooterV3Version uint16 = 3       // 22-byte footer (v10–v12 files)
FooterV3Size    uint   = 22
FooterV4Version uint16 = 4       // 34-byte footer (v13+ files); adds intrinsic index fields
FooterV4Size    uint   = 34

ColumnEncodingVersion uint8 = 2  // Per-column encoding header version byte
VersionBlockV12       uint8 = 12 // Block content version 12: column metadata omits 16-byte stats stub

CompactIndexVersion  uint8 = 1   // Compact trace index v1 (no bloom)
CompactIndexVersion2 uint8 = 2   // Compact trace index v2 (with bloom)

TraceIDBloomK            = 7     // Kirsch-Mitzenmacher hash count
TraceIDBloomBitsPerTrace = 10    // Bloom bits per trace ID (~0.8% FP rate with k=7)
TraceIDBloomMinBytes     = 128   // Minimum bloom filter size
TraceIDBloomMaxBytes     = 1<<20 // Maximum bloom filter size (1 MiB)

Limits

ConstantValueDescription
MaxSpans1,000,000Maximum spans per block
MaxBlocks65,535Maximum blocks per file (uint16 block ID in trace index)
MaxColumns10,000Maximum columns per block
MaxDictionarySize1,000,000Maximum dictionary entries per column
MaxStringLen10,485,760Maximum string/bytes value length (10 MiB)
MaxBytesLen10,485,760Maximum bytes value length (10 MiB)
MaxBlockSize1,073,741,824Maximum uncompressed block size (1 GiB)
MaxMetadataSize268,435,456Maximum metadata section size after decompression (256 MiB)
MaxTraceCount1,000,000Maximum unique traces per block
MaxNameLen1,024Maximum column/field name length in bytes
MaxCompactSectionSize52,428,800Maximum compact trace index size (50 MiB)
DefaultRangeBuckets1,000Default number of KLL quantile buckets per range column
RangeBucketKeyMaxLen50Maximum byte length of a RangeString/RangeBytes bucket key

Footer

Footer v3 (22 bytes) — files v10–v12

The footer occupies the last 22 bytes of every v10/v11/v12 blockpack file. Always read first.

FieldTypeBytesDescription
versionuint16 LE2Must equal 3
header_offsetuint64 LE8Absolute byte offset of the File Header
compact_offsetuint64 LE8Absolute byte offset of the Compact Trace Index
compact_lenuint32 LE4Byte length of the Compact Trace Index (0 = absent)

If compact_len == 0, the Compact Trace Index is absent and compact_offset is ignored.

Footer v4 (34 bytes) — files v13+

Extends footer v3 with two additional fields for the Intrinsic Index section:

FieldTypeBytesDescription
versionuint16 LE2Must equal 4
header_offsetuint64 LE8Absolute byte offset of the File Header
compact_offsetuint64 LE8Absolute byte offset of the Compact Trace Index
compact_lenuint32 LE4Byte length of the Compact Trace Index (0 = absent)
intrinsicindexoffsetuint64 LE8Absolute byte offset of the Intrinsic Index section
intrinsicindexlenuint32 LE4Byte length of the Intrinsic Index section (0 = absent)

Readers that encounter version 4 and do not support the intrinsic index MUST still be able to read the file; they simply ignore the intrinsic index fields.

File Header

Located at header_offset from the footer.

v10/v11 Header (21 bytes)

FieldTypeBytesOffsetDescription
magicuint32 LE40Must equal 0xC011FEA1
versionuint814File version: 10 or 11
metadata_offsetuint64 LE85Absolute byte offset of the Metadata Section
metadata_lenuint64 LE813Byte length of the Metadata Section

v12+ Header (22 bytes)

Identical to v10/v11 with one additional byte:

FieldTypeBytesOffsetDescription
magicuint32 LE40Must equal 0xC011FEA1
versionuint814Must equal 12 or 13
metadata_offsetuint64 LE85Absolute byte offset of the Metadata Section (compressed size)
metadata_lenuint64 LE813Byte length of the Metadata Section (compressed, on-disk size)
signal_typeuint81210x01 = trace spans, 0x02 = log records

In v12+, metadata_len is the compressed byte length. The metadata blob at metadata_offset must be snappy-decompressed before parsing. After decompression, readers MUST validate the decoded length does not exceed MaxMetadataSize (256 MiB).

Writers producing log files MUST write version 12+ and signal_type = 0x02.

Block Payloads

Each block payload is a self-contained binary blob located by the Block Index. Blocks are written in order starting at byte 0 of the file.

Block Header (24 bytes)

FieldTypeBytesOffsetDescription
magicuint32 LE40Must equal 0xC011FEA1
versionuint814Block content version: 10, 11, or 12
reserved[3]byte35Must be zero
span_countuint32 LE48Number of spans in this block
column_countuint32 LE412Number of columns in this block
reserved2[8]byte816Must be zero (formerly tracecount + tracetable_len)

Column Metadata Array

Immediately follows the block header. One entry per column, column_count entries total, sorted lexicographically by column name.

Block content version 10/11 — each ColumnMetadataEntry:

name_len     uint16 LE
name         [name_len]byte
col_type     uint8              // ColumnType (see Column Types section)
data_offset  uint64 LE          // Absolute byte offset of column data within block payload
data_len     uint64 LE          // Byte length of column data blob
reserved     [16]byte           // stats_offset[8] + stats_len[8] — always 0; retained for compat

Block content version 12 (written by file v13+ writers) — each ColumnMetadataEntry:

name_len     uint16 LE
name         [name_len]byte
col_type     uint8
data_offset  uint64 LE
data_len     uint64 LE
// reserved[16] omitted — saves 16 bytes per column per block

All data_offset values are relative to the first byte of the block payload (byte 0 = block magic).

Column Data Section

Immediately follows the Column Metadata Array. One encoded blob per column in the same order as the metadata entries. Each blob begins with the Column Encoding Header:

encoding_version  uint8    // Must equal 2
encoding_kind     uint8    // Determines remainder of wire format (see Column Encoding section)

Column Types

Used in the col_type field of Column Metadata entries and the Range Index.

IDNameGo TypeNotes
0StringstringMay be stored as 16-byte binary if UUID-shaped
1Int64int64
2Uint64uint64Used for timestamps (ns), counters
3Float64float64
4BoolboolStored as uint8 (0/1) internally
5Bytes[]byte
6RangeInt64int64Range-bucketed for high-cardinality indexing
7RangeUint64uint64Range-bucketed
8RangeDurationint64Duration in nanoseconds, range-bucketed
9RangeFloat64float64Range-bucketed
10RangeBytes[]byteRange-bucketed
11RangeStringstringRange-bucketed

Valid type byte range: 0–11. Values 12–255 are reserved. Range* types (6–11) appear in the Range Index; Bool columns do not appear in the Range Index.

Column Encoding

All 13 encoding kinds share a common 2-byte header followed by kind-specific wire format.

Dense vs Sparse Variants

Encoding kinds follow a pattern:

  • Odd kinds (1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13): dense — data for all span_count rows, including nulls
  • Even kinds (2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12): sparse — only data for rows where the presence bitset is 1

The writer selects sparse encoding when more than 50% of rows are null.

Encoding Kind Table

KindNameDense/SparseApplicable TypesWriter Status
1DictionaryDenseAll typesActive
2SparseDictionarySparseAll typesActive
3InlineBytesDenseBytesReader-only (legacy)
4SparseInlineBytesSparseBytesReader-only (legacy)
5DeltaUint64DenseUint64 (timestamps, counters)Active
6RLEIndexesDenseAny type with cardinality <= 3Active
7SparseRLEIndexesSparseSame, sparseActive
8XORBytesDenseBytes — ID columns (span:id, etc.)Active
9SparseXORBytesSparseBytes — ID columnsActive
10PrefixBytesDenseBytes — URL/path columnsActive
11SparsePrefixBytesSparseBytes — URL/pathActive
12DeltaDictionaryDenseBytes — trace:idActive
13SparseDeltaDictionarySparseBytes — trace:idActive

> Kinds 3/4 (InlineBytes) were emitted by earlier writer versions and remain decodable. The current writer never selects them — all bytes columns are encoded as XORBytes (8/9) or PrefixBytes (10/11).

Encoding Selection Rules

The writer applies the following rules to select an encoding kind for each column:

  1. RLEIndexes (6/7): column cardinality (distinct values) is 3 or fewer (boolean-like enums).
  2. DeltaUint64 (5): Uint64 columns (timestamps, counters). Dense only — no sparse variant.
  3. DeltaDictionary (12/13): trace:id Bytes column (spans sorted by trace ID produce sequential dictionary accesses, making delta encoding effective).
  4. XORBytes (8/9): other short fixed-length Bytes columns — ID columns (span:id, parent:id).
  5. PrefixBytes (10/11): Bytes columns detected as URLs or paths with shared common prefixes.
  6. Dictionary (1/2): all remaining types (String, Int64, Float64, Bool, Bytes).
  7. Sparse variant (even kind) is chosen when null ratio exceeds 50%.

Presence RLE (used by most kinds)

A compact bitset with RLE compression encoding which rows have a value:

rle_len      uint32 LE
rle_data     [rle_len]byte     // RLE-encoded presence bitset

The decompressed bitset has span_count bits. Bit i set means span row i has a value.

The RLE format within rle_data:

version      uint8 = 1
run_count    uint32 LE
[run_count × Run]

Each Run:

length       uint32 LE    // Number of consecutive same-value bits
value        uint32 LE    // 0 = absent, 1 = present

Kind 1/2 — Dictionary

Used for String, Int64, Uint64, Bool, Float64, Bytes. The dictionary is zstd-compressed.

index_width    uint8                    // 1, 2, or 4 (bytes per index value)
dict_len       uint32 LE               // Byte length of compressed dictionary
dict_data      [dict_len]byte           // zstd-compressed dictionary body
row_count      uint32 LE               // Must equal block span_count
presence_rle   [rle_len(4) + rle_data]
indexes        [see below]

Dictionary body after zstd decompression:

entry_count    uint32 LE
[entry_count × DictEntry]

DictEntry format by column type:

  • String: len(4 LE uint32) + string_bytes
  • Int64: value(8 LE int64)
  • Uint64: value(8 LE uint64)
  • Bool: value(1 uint8) — 0 or 1
  • Float64: bits(8 LE uint64) — IEEE 754
  • Bytes: len(4 LE uint32) + byte_data

Index array after presence RLE:

  • Dense (kind 1): row_count indexes, one per row including nulls. Each index is index_width bytes.
  • Sparse (kind 2): preceded by present_count(4 LE uint32), then present_count indexes for present rows only.

Kind 6/7 — RLE Indexes

Same header as Dictionary (indexwidth, dictlen, dictdata, rowcount, presence_rle), then instead of a raw index array, an RLE-compressed index sequence:

index_count    uint32 LE         // Total number of index values
rle_len        uint32 LE         // Byte length of RLE data
rle_data       [rle_len]byte     // Index RLE: version(1) + run_count(4) + runs

Each run contains the index value repeated length times. Applicable when column cardinality is 3 or fewer (boolean-like enums).

Kind 3/4 — Inline Bytes (reader-only legacy)

Each value stored directly, no dictionary. The current writer never emits these kinds. Readers MUST be able to decode them.

row_count      uint32 LE
presence_rle   [rle_len(4) + rle_data]

Dense (kind 3):

[row_count × (len(4 LE uint32) + byte_data)]

Sparse (kind 4):

present_count  uint32 LE
[present_count × (len(4 LE uint32) + byte_data)]

Kind 5 — Delta Uint64

Uint64 values encoded as offsets from a base (minimum) value. Applied to timestamp and monotonic counter columns.

span_count     uint32 LE
presence_rle   [rle_len(4) + rle_data]
base_value     uint64 LE        // Minimum value in block (absolute)
width          uint8            // Bytes per offset: 0 (no values), 1, 2, 4, or 8

If width == 0, no offsets follow. Otherwise:

offset_len     uint32 LE
offsets_zstd   [offset_len]byte  // zstd-compressed offset array

After decompression, width bytes per present row in order. Reconstructed value: base_value + offset.

Kind 8/9 — XOR Bytes

Sequential byte values XOR'd against the previous value. Used for ID columns (span:id, parent:id) which share common high bytes.

span_count     uint32 LE
presence_rle   [rle_len(4) + rle_data]
xor_len        uint32 LE
xor_data_zstd  [xor_len]byte

XOR payload after decompression: for each present row in order:

val_len        uint32 LE
xor_bytes      [val_len]byte    // XOR with previous present value; first value stored raw

Decode: value[i] = xor_bytes[i] XOR value[i-1] (byte-wise, up to min length; extra bytes appended as-is). Sparse variant (kind 9) is identical — sparseness is only in the presence bitset.

Kind 10/11 — Prefix Bytes

Byte values stored as (prefix_index, suffix) pairs. Suitable for URLs and paths with shared common prefixes.

span_count          uint32 LE
presence_rle        [rle_len(4) + rle_data]

prefix_dict_len     uint32 LE
prefix_dict_zstd    [prefix_dict_len]byte   // zstd-compressed prefix dictionary

suffix_data_len     uint32 LE
suffix_data_zstd    [suffix_data_len]byte   // zstd-compressed suffix section

Prefix dictionary after decompression:

prefix_count   uint32 LE
[prefix_count × (len(4 LE uint32) + prefix_bytes)]

Suffix section after decompression:

prefix_index_width  uint8           // 1, 2, or 4 bytes per prefix index
[per present row × SuffixEntry]

Each SuffixEntry:

prefix_idx     [prefix_index_width bytes LE]   // Index into prefix dict; 0xFFFFFFFF = no prefix
suffix_len     uint32 LE
suffix_bytes   [suffix_len]byte

Reconstructed value: prefix_dict[prefix_idx] + suffix_bytes. If prefix_idx equals the sentinel (0xFF, 0xFFFF, or 0xFFFFFFFF depending on width), value is suffix_bytes only.

Kind 12/13 — Delta Dictionary Bytes

Bytes dictionary with delta-encoded indexes. Optimized for trace:id where spans are sorted, making adjacent rows reference nearby dictionary entries.

index_width         uint8            // Present but unused for delta decoding; always read
dict_len            uint32 LE
dict_data_zstd      [dict_len]byte   // zstd-compressed dictionary (Bytes dict format from §Dictionary)
row_count           uint32 LE
presence_rle        [rle_len(4) + rle_data]
delta_len           uint32 LE
delta_data_zstd     [delta_len]byte  // zstd-compressed delta-encoded index array

Delta array after decompression: one int32 LE per row (dense, kind 12) or per present row (sparse, kind 13).

Decode: accumulate deltas from 0. index[i] = prev + delta[i]. Result must be in [0, dict_size).

Metadata Section

Located at metadata_offset, length metadata_len. For v12+ files, this blob is snappy-compressed on disk — decompress it before parsing. Sub-sections appear in the following order:

  1. Block Index
  2. Range Index
  3. Column Index stub (always empty)
  4. Trace Block Index
  5. TS Index (optional, new files only)
  6. Sketch Index (optional)
  7. File Bloom (FBLM) section (optional)

Block Index

Parsed at open time. offset and length drive all block I/O. MinStart/MaxStart prune by time range. MinTraceID/MaxTraceID prune by trace ID (v10/v11/v12 only).

block_count    uint32 LE
[block_count × blockIndexEntry]

Block Index Entry — v10

FieldTypeBytesDescription
offsetuint64 LE8Absolute file offset of block payload
lengthuint64 LE8Byte length of block payload
span_countuint32 LE4Number of spans in the block
min_startuint64 LE8Minimum span start timestamp (nanoseconds)
max_startuint64 LE8Maximum span start timestamp (nanoseconds)
mintraceid[16]byte16Minimum trace ID (lexicographic)
maxtraceid[16]byte16Maximum trace ID (lexicographic)

Total: 68 bytes per entry. (column_name_bloom[32] was removed 2026-03-07; old total was 100 bytes.)

Block Index Entry — v11/v12 addition

A single kind byte is inserted after length and before span_count:

FieldTypeBytesDescription
offsetuint64 LE8
lengthuint64 LE8
kinduint81Block entry kind: 0 = leaf (only value in use)
span_countuint32 LE4
min_startuint64 LE8
max_startuint64 LE8
mintraceid[16]byte16
maxtraceid[16]byte16

Total: 69 bytes per entry.

Block Index Entry — v13 change

v13 omits min_trace_id and max_trace_id entirely (saves 32 bytes per entry):

FieldTypeBytesDescription
offsetuint64 LE8
lengthuint64 LE8
kinduint81Block entry kind: 0 = leaf
span_countuint32 LE4
min_startuint64 LE8
max_startuint64 LE8

Total: 37 bytes per entry.

Range Index

An inverted index mapping value bucket keys to block IDs. Used for range predicate pruning before any I/O. Immediately follows the block index.

range_count    uint32 LE       // Number of range columns
[range_count × RangeColumnEntry]

RangeColumnEntry

Every column except trace:id and Bool columns MUST appear. column_type must be one of the Range* types (IDs 6–11).

name_len       uint16 LE
name           [name_len]byte
column_type    uint8              // ColumnType (Range* only)

// Bucket metadata (always present)
bucket_min     int64 LE           // Minimum value across all blocks
bucket_max     int64 LE           // Maximum value across all blocks
boundary_count uint32 LE
boundaries     [boundary_count × int64 LE]   // KLL quantile boundaries

typed_count    uint32 LE          // Type-specific boundary count
[typed boundaries — format depends on column_type]

Typed boundary formats after typed_count:

  • RangeFloat64: typed_count × float64_bits(8 LE uint64)
  • RangeString: typed_count × (len(4 LE uint32) + string_bytes)
  • RangeBytes: typed_count × (len(4 LE uint32) + byte_data)
  • RangeInt64 / RangeUint64 / RangeDuration: typed_count must be 0; no additional bytes

Then the value list:

value_count    uint32 LE
[value_count × RangeValueEntry]

RangeValueEntry

value_key      [type-specific encoding]     // Lower boundary of the bucket
block_id_count uint32 LE
block_ids      [block_id_count × uint32 LE] // Sorted block IDs in this bucket

Value keys use the lower boundary of the KLL bucket, not a compact ID. This makes the index self-describing and supports binary search without loading full bucket metadata.

Value Key Wire Format by Column Type

ColumnTypeKey Wire Format
RangeInt64, RangeDurationlength_prefix(1 uint8)=8 + int64_LE(8)
RangeUint64length_prefix(1 uint8)=8 + uint64_LE(8)
RangeFloat64length_prefix(1 uint8)=8 + float64_bits_LE(8)
RangeStringlen(4 LE uint32) + key_data — boundary string truncated to min(len, 50) bytes
RangeByteslen(4 LE uint32) + key_data — boundary bytes truncated to min(len, 50) bytes

Value keys within a column are stored in lexicographic sort order.

Range Index Lookup Semantics

To find blocks for a query value V:

  1. Encode V using the key wire format above.
  2. Binary-search stored entries (sorted by ascending lower boundary) for the entry with the largest lower boundary <= encoded(V).
  3. Return that entry's block IDs. If no entry satisfies the condition, return empty.

For numeric types (RangeInt64, RangeUint64, RangeFloat64, RangeDuration) the 8-byte LE key is decoded to its numeric type before comparison. For RangeString/RangeBytes, raw byte comparison is used.

For interval predicates like duration > X, use interval matching: a block is retained if its bucket range overlaps [X, maxSentinel]. Sentinels: math.MaxUint64 for uint64, math.MaxInt64 for int64, +Inf for float64.

Column Index Stub

Always empty. Written as one col_count[4 LE] = 0 per block. Retained for format compatibility. Immediately follows the Range Index.

Trace Block Index

Sorted mapping of trace ID to the block IDs that contain spans from that trace. Used by FindTraceByID for O(log N) binary search.

Two format versions:

Version 1 (legacy):

format_version uint8         // 0x01
trace_count    uint32 LE
[trace_count × TraceEntry]   // Sorted by trace_id (lexicographic)

Each v1 TraceEntry:

trace_id       [16]byte
block_count    uint16 LE
[block_count × { block_id(uint16 LE) · span_count(uint16 LE) · span_indices(span_count × uint16 LE) }]

Version 2 (current):

format_version uint8         // 0x02
trace_count    uint32 LE
[trace_count × TraceEntry]   // Sorted by trace_id (lexicographic)

Each v2 TraceEntry:

trace_id       [16]byte
block_count    uint16 LE
block_ids      [block_count × uint16 LE]  // Block indices; no span indices stored

Span indices are not stored in v2. The reader scans the trace:id column in the already-fetched block to find matching rows (blocks are always read fully in one I/O, so scanning is pure CPU with no additional I/O cost).

Log files write the trace block index with trace_count = 0 (valid per the format).

TS Index

Optional section appended after the Trace Block Index. Written by all new files (2026-03-02 onward). Provides O(log N) binary search for blocks overlapping a time window.

magic[4 LE]   = 0xC011FEED
version[1]    = 1
count[4 LE]   // Number of entries (one per block)
[count × entry]  // Sorted by min_ts ascending

Each entry (20 bytes):

min_ts[8 LE]     // BlockMeta.MinStart (Unix nanoseconds)
max_ts[8 LE]     // BlockMeta.MaxStart (Unix nanoseconds)
block_id[4 LE]   // 0-based index into block index array

Overlap condition for query window [queryMin, queryMax]:

block.max_ts >= queryMin  AND  block.min_ts <= queryMax

Blocks with min_ts == max_ts == 0 (unknown time) are always included. Old readers that lack TS Index awareness ignore trailing bytes in the metadata section gracefully.

Sketch Index

Optional section written after the TS Index. Present only when blocks exist. Contains per-column HLL cardinality estimates, TopK frequency data, Count-Min Sketch (CMS) frequency tables, and BinaryFuse8 filters. Stored in column-major order.

magic[4 LE]      = 0x534B5443  // "SKTC"
num_blocks[4 LE]
num_columns[4 LE]

[num_columns × ColumnSketch]   // Sorted by column name

Each ColumnSketch:

col_name_len[2 LE]
col_name[col_name_len]

// Presence bitset: which blocks have this column
presence[ceil(num_blocks / 8) bytes]   // bit i = 1 means block i has this column

// HLL cardinality: one uint32 per block
distinct[num_blocks × 4 LE]   // 0 if column absent in that block

// TopK
topk_k[1] = 20
// For each present block (in block order):
  entry_count[1]
  [entry_count × { fp[8 LE] · count[2 LE] }]  // fp = fingerprint of value

// CMS
cms_depth[1] = 4
cms_width[2 LE] = 64
// For each present block: cms_data[depth × width × 2] = 512 bytes

// BinaryFuse8 per block
// For each present block: fuse_len[4 LE] · fuse_data[fuse_len]

HLL (HyperLogLog): approximate distinct value count per column per block. Used for cardinality-based query planning.

TopK: stores the 20 most frequent values (as fingerprints) per column per block with approximate counts. Used to prioritize blocks likely to contain frequently queried values.

CMS (Count-Min Sketch): 4-row × 64-column sketch (512 bytes per block per column). Answers "roughly how many times does value X appear?" in O(1). Used for block scoring: a CMS score of zero for all queried values allows skipping that block.

BinaryFuse8: probabilistic membership filter (~0.39% false positive rate, ~1 byte per entry). Used at query time for exact-value block pruning: if the queried value is not in the filter, the block is skipped.

File Bloom (FBLM)

Optional section appended after the Sketch Index in the metadata blob. Stores a BinaryFuse8 filter for resource.service.name values, enabling O(1) file-level rejection for service-name equality predicates without opening any block payload.

magic[4 LE]     = 0x46424C4D  // "FBLM"
version[1]      = 0x01
col_count[4 LE]

[col_count × entry]  // Sorted by column name

Each entry:

name_len[2 LE]
name[name_len]
fuse_len[4 LE]
fuse_data[fuse_len]   // BinaryFuse8 filter bytes; fuse_len=0 means no data (always pass)

col_count is bounded to <= 1000. fuse_len = 0 is a conservative pass (no false negatives introduced). Old readers that do not parse this section ignore trailing metadata bytes without corruption.

Compact Trace Index

Located at compact_offset, length compact_len (from footer). Absent when compact_len == 0. Provides a self-contained trace lookup structure that can be used without parsing the full Metadata Section.

Version 1 (legacy, no bloom)

magic[4 LE]     = 0xC01DC1DE
version[1]      = 1
block_count[4 LE]
block_table[block_count × CompactBlockEntry]
format_version[1] = 0x01
trace_count[4 LE]
[trace_count × TraceEntry v1]

Version 2 (current, with trace ID bloom)

magic[4 LE]     = 0xC01DC1DE
version[1]      = 2
block_count[4 LE]
bloom_bytes[4 LE]
bloom_data[bloom_bytes]          // Trace ID bloom filter
block_table[block_count × CompactBlockEntry]
format_version[1] = 0x02
trace_count[4 LE]
[trace_count × TraceEntry v2]    // Same format as Trace Block Index §v2

Each CompactBlockEntry (12 bytes):

file_offset[8 LE]   // Absolute file byte offset of block payload
file_length[4 LE]   // Byte length of block payload

The trace ID bloom filter allows immediate rejection of absent trace IDs without allocating or searching the trace index hash map. Size formula: max(128, min(1<<20, n * 10 / 8)) bytes where n = distinct trace count. Hash algorithm: Kirsch-Mitzenmacher double-hashing using the trace ID bytes directly as entropy.

Readers that encounter an unknown compact index version must return an error.

Intrinsic Index (v13+ / Footer v4)

The Intrinsic Index is a file-level columnar section that aggregates key intrinsic columns (e.g., span:start, span:name, resource.service.name) across all blocks into a compact file-level structure. Located at intrinsic_index_offset in the footer v4. Absent when intrinsic_index_len == 0.

The section begins with a snappy-compressed TOC blob followed by per-column data pages. The TOC wire format:

toc_version[1] = 1
col_count[4 LE]
[col_count × IntrinsicColMeta]

Each IntrinsicColMeta:

name_len[2 LE]
name[name_len]
col_type[1]           // ColumnType
format[1]             // 0x01 = flat array, 0x02 = dictionary
offset[8 LE]          // Absolute file offset of column data
length[4 LE]          // Byte length of column data
entry_count[4 LE]     // Number of values in column
min_len[2 LE]         // Byte length of minimum value (encoded)
min_val[min_len]
max_len[2 LE]         // Byte length of maximum value (encoded)
max_val[max_len]

Each column blob is either:

  • Flat (format 0x01): delta-encoded uint64 array (for Uint64 columns) or length-prefixed bytes (for Bytes/String).
  • Dictionary (format 0x02): compact dictionary encoding.
  • Paged (version byte 0x02 at blob start): v2 paged format with TOC + page blobs, used when rows exceed IntrinsicPageSize (10,000). Each page blob is independently decodable and may carry a per-page BinaryFuse8 bloom filter.

Indexes and Filters

Each index structure is consulted during query planning before any block payload bytes are fetched.

Block Index

Maps each block to its byte offset and size. Loaded once at file open. Every block fetch uses this index to locate the byte range.

Trace Block Index

Sorted array of (traceID, []blockID) pairs. Enables O(log N) binary search for trace-by-ID lookups without reading any block payloads. Used by FindTraceByID.

Range Index (KLL-Derived)

At write time, a KLL sketch (Karnin-Lang-Liberty) is built per numeric and string column across all blocks. The sketch computes approximately DefaultRangeBuckets = 1000 quantile bucket boundaries for the file. Each block's contribution is recorded as the set of KLL buckets whose range overlaps that block's [min, max] values for that column. The KLL sketch itself is not stored on disk — only the derived bucket boundaries and value-to-block mappings.

At query time, the planner uses interval matching: for a predicate duration > X, a block is retained only if its recorded bucket range overlaps [X, MaxSentinel]. Blocks with no overlapping buckets are pruned without any I/O.

Bloom Filters

Blockpack uses BinaryFuse8 filters — a space-efficient probabilistic structure that answers "does value X exist in this set?" with ~0.39% false positive rate and ~1 byte per entry. No false negatives are possible.

There are two levels:

File-level bloom (FBLM section): Covers resource.service.name across all spans in the file. If the queried service is absent, zero blocks are read and the query returns immediately.

Block-level bloom (Sketch Index per column): One BinaryFuse8 filter per column per block, over the column's distinct values. Used during pruning to reject blocks that cannot contain a queried value. False positives cause unnecessary block reads; false negatives are impossible.

Column Name Bloom (removed 2026-03-07): The column_name_bloom[32] field in Block Index entries was removed. The Sketch Index CMS subsumes column presence: an absent column has CMS count 0, which the planner treats conservatively (block passes).

Trace ID Bloom Filter

Stored in the Compact Trace Index (version 2). Encodes all trace IDs in the file. Allows BlocksForTraceIDCompact to return nil for absent trace IDs without hash map allocation.

Hash algorithm: Kirsch-Mitzenmacher double-hashing. h1 = LE uint64(traceID[0:8]), h2 = LE uint64(traceID[8:16]) | 1 (force odd stride). For each of k=7 hash functions: pos = (h1 + i*h2) mod (m*8), set/test bloom[pos/8] bit (pos%8). Trace IDs are random 128-bit values and serve as their own entropy.

TS Index

Sorted array of (min_ts, max_ts, block_id) entries (20 bytes each) enabling O(log N) binary search for blocks overlapping a time window. Written by all new files; old files without this section degrade gracefully.

CMS (Count-Min Sketch)

A 4-row × 64-column sketch per column per block. Answers "roughly how many times does value X appear in this block?" in O(1) and constant space. Blocks with CMS score of zero for all queried values can be skipped. Blocks with high scores are prioritized for early termination.

Column Naming Convention

Column names use a two-part convention separating OTLP intrinsics from user-defined attributes:

SeparatorMeaningExamples
: (colon)OTLP intrinsic field — fixed, well-known field from OTLP protospan:name, trace:id, span:duration
. (dot)User-defined attribute — key from OTLP attributes map, prefixed by scopespan.http.method, resource.service.name, scope.library.name

A writer MUST NOT use : in user attribute columns, and MUST NOT use . in OTLP intrinsic columns.

Trace Intrinsic Columns

Column NameTypeSource
trace:idBytesSpan.TraceId
trace:stateStringSpan.TraceState
span:idBytesSpan.SpanId
span:parent_idBytesSpan.ParentSpanId
span:nameStringSpan.Name
span:kindInt64Span.Kind
span:startUint64Span.StartTimeUnixNano
span:endUint64Span.EndTimeUnixNano
span:durationUint64EndTimeUnixNano - StartTimeUnixNano
span:statusInt64Span.Status.Code
span:status_messageStringSpan.Status.Message
span:dropped_attrsUint64Span.DroppedAttributesCount
span:dropped_eventsUint64Span.DroppedEventsCount
span:dropped_linksUint64Span.DroppedLinksCount
resource:schema_urlStringResourceSpans.SchemaUrl
scope:schema_urlStringScopeSpans.SchemaUrl

Log Intrinsic Columns (v12+, signal_type=0x02)

Column NameTypeSourceNotes
log:timestampUint64LogRecord.TimeUnixNanoAlways present
log:observed_timestampUint64LogRecord.ObservedTimeUnixNanoAlways present
log:bodyStringLogRecord.BodyAlways present
log:severity_numberInt64LogRecord.SeverityNumberNull when zero
log:severity_textStringLogRecord.SeverityTextNull when empty
log:trace_idBytesLogRecord.TraceIdNull when absent
log:span_idBytesLogRecord.SpanIdNull when absent
log:flagsUint64LogRecord.FlagsNull when zero

Log files sort spans by (resource.service.name ASC, MinHashSig ASC, log:timestamp ASC). The Trace Block Index section is present but empty (trace_count = 0). MinStart/MaxStart in block metadata carry min/max log:timestamp for time-range pruning.

User-Attribute Column Names

PatternSource
resource.{key}Resource.Attributes[key] (traces and logs)
span.{key}Span.Attributes[key] (traces only)
scope.{key}Scope.Attributes[key]
log.{key}LogRecord.Attributes[key] or auto-parsed body field

Auto-Parsed Log Body Columns

When a log body is a JSON object or logfmt string, the writer auto-parses all top-level key-value pairs and stores them as additional log.{key} columns. These body-parsed columns use ColumnTypeRangeString (type ID 11), enabling min/max block-level pruning. Explicit LogRecord.Attributes use ColumnTypeString (type ID 0).

If the same key appears in both body-parse and explicit attributes, both columns are stored separately (the ColumnKey type includes the column type, preventing collision). No deduplication occurs at ingest.

Body parsing failures produce no error and no extra columns. The log:body intrinsic is always stored.

I/O Design

Blockpack targets object storage (S3, GCS, Azure Blob) where request latency (50-100ms) dominates cost, not bytes transferred.

> One I/O per block — always read the entire block.

Per-column selective reads were tried and removed: they caused 10-120x more API calls with no meaningful reduction in bytes transferred. All column filtering happens in-memory after the single block fetch.

MetricGoodWarningCritical
io_ops< 500500-1000> 1000
bytes/io> 100KB10-100KB< 10KB

Streaming Write Invariant

A compliant writer MUST:

  1. Write block payloads to the output stream immediately as each block is finalized.
  2. Record the absolute byte offset and length of each block as it is written.
  3. After all blocks: append File Header, Metadata Section, Compact Trace Index (if any), Intrinsic Index (if any), Footer in that order.
  4. Never seek backwards.

Source

  • Specification: internal/modules/blockio/SPECS.md
  • Writer: internal/modules/blockio/writer/
  • Reader: internal/modules/blockio/reader/
  • Shared types: internal/modules/blockio/shared/
